skill.name: frontend-design
skill.description: Guides the creation of distinctive, production-ready frontend interfaces with strong visual direction and refined implementation details.
skill.summary: Create memorable frontend interfaces that avoid generic AI design patterns.
skill.author: DennisLiuCk

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography findings at SKILL.md lines 2 and 3 are false positives from plain metadata and descriptive text. The entropy finding at line 1 is also false because the file is readable Markdown, and no prompt injection, code execution, network, filesystem, or secret access behavior was found.

low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is visible frontmatter with the name frontend-design. No cryptographic code or algorithm selection appears there.

low_findings.description: The finding at SKILL.md line 3 is a false positive. The line describes frontend design behavior and does not reference cryptography.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is natural-language skill metadata. I found no encryption, hashing, or security-sensitive code in this location.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: High Entropy Content
low_findings.description: The entropy finding at SKILL.md line 1 is a false positive. The file is plain Markdown frontmatter and contains no binary or encrypted payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 1 is the standard Markdown frontmatter delimiter. The full scanned file is readable text with no obfuscated payload.

content.value_statement: Generic AI interfaces often feel repetitive and weak. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward distinctive, production-ready frontend work.
actual_capabilities: Encourages production-ready HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, or Vue interfaces.
Promotes deliberate typography, color, motion, spacing, and background choices.
Helps avoid common AI design defaults and repeated component patterns.
Adapts visual complexity to the chosen design concept.
limitations: Provides guidance only; quality depends on model and project context.
Does not include reusable code files, assets, or automated tests.
Does not verify accessibility, browser behavior, or performance by itself.
May need extra constraints for strict brand systems or enterprise design systems.

use_cases.title: Design a Product Page
use_cases.description: Create a polished product page with a clear aesthetic concept and production-ready frontend implementation.

use_cases.title: Improve an Internal Tool
use_cases.description: Turn a basic dashboard or workflow screen into a usable interface with stronger hierarchy and visual detail.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Creative Web Experience
use_cases.description: Explore a bold visual direction for a campaign, portfolio, or editorial page before formal design work begins.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to build a landing page for [product]. Audience: [audience]. Tone: [tone]. Include responsive HTML and CSS.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a complete page.

prompt_templates.title: Redesign an Existing Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to redesign this interface: [describe screen]. Keep the workflow intact, but improve layout, typography, color, and motion.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Branded Component Set
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to create a cohesive component set for [brand]. Include buttons, cards, navigation, forms, and empty states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for reusable interface parts.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Distinctive App Experience
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-design skill to design and implement a full app interface for [domain]. Choose a strong aesthetic direction and explain key choices.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for concept, design, and implementation.

output_examples.input: Build a homepage for a boutique analytics product.
output_examples.output: A responsive homepage with refined typography, a focused hero, product proof points, pricing cues, and motion that supports the brand tone.

output_examples.input: Redesign a project dashboard for daily use.
output_examples.output: A denser operational layout with clearer task priority, stronger spacing, accessible contrast, and subtle interaction states.

output_examples.input: Create a portfolio page for an experimental designer.
output_examples.output: A memorable portfolio concept with expressive type, asymmetric composition, project previews, and responsive visual polish.

best_practices: Provide the audience, product purpose, and technical stack before asking for implementation.
Name brand constraints, accessibility goals, and performance limits early.
Ask for a clear aesthetic direction before requesting final code.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a beautiful page without explaining the user or business goal.
Do not accept generic gradients, default fonts, and repeated card layouts without critique.
Do not skip responsive behavior, interaction states, or accessibility requirements.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create distinctive frontend interfaces for pages, components, and web applications.

faq.question: Does it generate working code?
faq.answer: Yes, it instructs the model to produce working frontend code when the user requests implementation.

faq.question: Which frameworks can it support?
faq.answer: It can guide HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, Vue, and similar frontend work.

faq.question: Is it a design system?
faq.answer: No. It is guidance for generating strong frontend designs, not a packaged design system.

faq.question: Can it follow an existing brand?
faq.answer: Yes, but the user should provide brand rules, examples, colors, typography, and accessibility needs.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file is plain Markdown guidance with no confirmed dangerous behavior.
